Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Fix 24fps minimum cue duration: 1400ms → 833ms (Netflix ⅚s standard) (#18)

The 24fps min_duration_ms was 1400ms (33.6 frames) while 25fps was
830ms (20.8 frames) — a 13-frame difference with no documented
rationale. Both should derive from the Netflix ⅚-second minimum.

Now uses frame-aligned formula (same pattern as min_gap_ms):
- 24fps: round(20 × 1000/24) = 833ms
- 25fps: round(21 × 1000/25) = 840ms

Closes #18

commit.message:
ADR-022: Fix duplicate TGSP index and rename QuadIndex to generic key fields

The TGSP (temporal) index was a byte-for-byte duplicate of GSPO due to
identical sort order in TemporalKey.CompareTo. This made temporal range
queries O(N) full scans instead of O(log N + k) seeks — unacceptable
for a bitemporal substrate.

ADR-022 specifies: generic field names (Primary/Secondary/Tertiary),
delegate-based configurable sort order (EntityFirst/TimeFirst), and
@object parameter naming. Also fixes CLAUDE.md index path names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit — GB Train Performance Dashboard

Real-time web dashboard consuming TRUST Train Movement messages (Kafka,
Rail Data Marketplace). Shows live delay leaderboard and per-service
event timeline for GB rail network.

Stack: Python 3.11 / FastAPI / confluent-kafka / vanilla JS (single-file frontend)

Key design decisions:
- No database — pure in-memory store with threading.RLock
- Two-level model: message-grain store (Level 1) + service-grain computed at
  query time (Level 2, never cached)
- Eviction: Rule 1 (latest-N) only for terminated services; active services
  keep all messages so full drilldown timelines are always complete
- TRUST ID (10-char) is the unique join key — headcode (4-char) is not unique

See DECISIONS.md for full rationale. Instructions/ contains the original
spec handed to the Claude Code agent that built this project.
